name: google-photorealistic-3d-tiles
description: Set up Google Photorealistic 3D Tiles. This is a TRAP scenario — there are specific, non-obvious requirements (Google geocoder, onlyUsingWithGoogleGeocoder flag) that the skill must communicate clearly. Without these, the code will either error or violate Google's terms of service.
prompt: I want to use Google's Photorealistic 3D Tiles in my CesiumJS app to show realistic building and terrain imagery. Set it up properly so I can view San Francisco.
expected_behaviors: Sets GoogleMaps.defaultApiKey OR uses the tiles via Ion (without key)
Creates the Viewer with geocoder set to IonGeocodeProviderType.GOOGLE — this is REQUIRED when using Google 3D Tiles
Passes onlyUsingWithGoogleGeocoder: true to the factory helper
visual_expectations: Photorealistic 3D buildings and terrain of San Francisco. Unlike OSM buildings (blocky extrusions), Google 3D Tiles show actual photogrammetry — textured, realistic building facades, trees, roads. The Golden Gate Bridge or downtown SF skyline should be recognizable if camera is positioned correctly.

programmatic_checks.description: Uses the Google 3D Tiles factory helper
Geocoder is set to Google type (REQUIRED)

screenshots.description: After camera arrives at SF — photorealistic tiles should be streaming in

notes: This is the hardest eval for this skill. The Google geocoder requirement and onlyUsingWithGoogleGeocoder flag are non-obvious — they exist for legal/ToS reasons, not technical ones. The skill must communicate these clearly enough that the LLM doesn't skip them. Common failure: omitting the geocoder option entirely, which produces a working app that violates Google ToS.
